prompt: Give me a short pep talk before I start a hard task I have been putting off all morning.
response:
Here's a short pep talk to get you going:

You've got this! You've been putting off this task all morning, but that's okay. It takes courage to acknowledge when we need a little extra motivation. Take a deep breath, stand up straight, and remind yourself that you're capable of crushing this task.

You've already made it through the hardest part - getting to this moment. Now it's time to take action. Break it down into smaller, manageable chunks if you need to. Focus on one step at a time. You're not trying to tackle the entire task at once, just the next small piece.

Remember why you wanted to tackle this task in the first place. What's the payoff? What benefits will you gain by completing it? Visualize yourself feeling accomplished and proud of what you've achieved.

You've overcome procrastination and shown up to the table. Now it's time to take ownership of this task and make progress. You're strong, you're capable, and you're ready to get it done.

So here's the deal. You're going to take a deep breath, grab a cup of coffee (or water, or whatever you need), and dive in. You're going to make progress, and you're going to feel good about it. Let's do this!
